overview: Bant Gearhulk leverages three-color flexibility to establish early board presence while ramping into high-value card advantage engines. By stabilizing with efficient removal and draw spells, the deck transitions into a late-game grind where massive creatures and recurring threats overwhelm opponents. The strategy prioritizes resource accumulation over early aggression, aiming to outlast and outvalue opposing decks in Standard.
win_conditions: Outvaluing opponents through Quantum Riddler's card draw and removal
Deploying large, resilient creatures that bypass early-game disruption
Generating excess mana with Nature's Rhythm to cast multiple threats per turn

keep_criteria: Hands with early mana sources and a one or two-drop
Draw spells or removal to stabilize against aggressive starts
Nature's Rhythm paired with a creature to accelerate curve

Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetches or duals that can find basics/Nature's Rhythm), a discard spell (Thoughtseize/Inquisition), and a turn-2 play (Badgermole Cub, Quantum Riddler, or cheap removal).
Keep hands that can play a land, cast a spell, and have a clear plan for turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early mana acceleration or interaction
Three or more high-cost cards without ramp
Hands missing a clear path to turn three or four
Mulligan flood, no lands, or all lands with no early interaction or threats.

early_game: Play lands and low-cost creatures to establish board presence
Use early interaction to disrupt opponent's game plan
Prioritize drawing cards to find ramp and threats
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize/Inquisition on a key threat or combo piece.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast a cheap creature (Badgermole Cub/Quantum Riddler) or follow up with removal.
If opening with Nature's Rhythm, play it on turn 1 or 2 for mana fixing and potential sacrifice for early damage or mana.
Sequence lands to ensure colored mana for interaction and avoid tapping out unnecessarily.
mid_game: Cast Nature's Rhythm to accelerate mana production
Deploy Quantum Riddler to generate card advantage and remove blockers
Maintain board control while searching for win conditions
Preserve hand for key interaction (discard/removal) until turn 3-4.
Use Nature's Rhythm to fix mana or generate extra mana for threats.
Bant's creatures are value engines, not pure attackers.
Manage graveyard by avoiding unnecessary sacrifices unless it fuels Aethergeode Titan/Gearhulk or enables Quantum Riddler.
Commit threats when you have removal for their response or when you can protect them with Teferi's Protection.
late_game: Cast large creatures that pressure the opponent's life total
Use excess mana to cast multiple spells per turn
Stabilize against late-game threats with removal and card draw

Aethergeode Titan or Gearhulk (exiling multiple creatures from graveyard for massive value), Teferi, Hero of Dominaria (card advantage and board wipe), or a well-timed Teferi's Protection on a key threat.
Win by out-grinding them with card advantage and efficient removal.
When you have card advantage in hand, removal for their threats, and they have no sweepers.
Also commit when you can safely cast a Gearhulk/Aethergeode Titan with multiple exiled creatures.
Go all-in when you can protect your threats with Teferi's Protection or when the opponent is out of interaction.

key_cards.name: Badgermole Cub
key_cards.role: Early-game board presence and token generation
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to establish early pressure and generate tokens that can block or trigger abilities. Use its low cost to stabilize against aggressive decks.

key_cards.name: Quantum Riddler
key_cards.notes: Prioritize casting when you have excess cards or need to clear a blocker. Use its draw ability to refill your hand and find answers, maintaining resource superiority.

key_cards.name: Nature's Rhythm
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn three or four to enable larger threats ahead of schedule. Ensure you have enough lands to support the extra mana, and use the generated mana to cast multiple spells or play a large creature.

sequencing_tips: Hold up removal for opponent's key threats before committing to your own plays
Cast Nature's Rhythm before playing large creatures to ensure you have enough mana
Use Quantum Riddler's draw ability to find answers before making a major board commitment
Prioritize playing lands and ramp before committing to high-cost spells
Common mistake: Overcommitting creatures without protection or removal.
Don't sacrifice Nature's Rhythm unnecessarily unless it's for mana or early damage.
Remember that Badgermole Cub and Quantum Riddler are value engines, not just attackers.
Use Teferi's Protection sparingly for maximum impact.
Watch for Izzet's sweepers and Landfall triggers.
The deck's strength is its resilience and card advantage, so play patiently and out-value opponents.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early interaction and board presence to slow down their early pressure. Use Badgermole Cub to generate blockers and stabilize the board.
Play around their removal by holding up counterspells or using Quantum Riddler to draw into answers. Ramp efficiently to outvalue them in the late game.
Race them by deploying threats quickly while maintaining card advantage. Use Nature's Rhythm to accelerate into larger creatures before they stabilize.

Use discard to remove counterspells early. Cast threats in response to their spells or when they're tapped out. Use Teferi's Protection to protect key threats. Don't overcommit to spells they can counter; use creatures that can be cast at instant speed or in response, and prioritize value engines that can't be easily countered.

Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development. Use discard to remove key ramp pieces. Stabilize with removal and Teferi's Protection, then out-value them with card advantage. Don't let them get ahead on mana; trade efficiently and punish their overextension.

Win the discard war. Prioritize keeping interaction. Use Teferi's Protection to protect key threats. Trade efficiently and win by out-resourcing with better late-game threats (Aethergeode Titan/Gearhulk). Don't overcommit to board position; focus on card advantage and graveyard value.

common_threats: Watch for Izzet's counterspells, sweepers (Teferi's Protection, Lightning Strike, Izzet Charm), and Landfall triggers.
Track when they run out of removal or discard.
Key tell: If they tap out for a big spell or creature without answering your early threats, they're vulnerable to pressure.
Note when they commit to a combo or ramp strategy to adjust your interaction accordingly.
